rationale: Because the synthetic ensures has the form `(big_conj) ==> det_pop_equal(...)`, the per-run ensures are not direct hypotheses in the body; I discharge the implication with `assert(... ==> ...) by { if (big_conj) { ... } }` so the two `forall|v:T| post_i@.contains(v) ==> pre_self_.get_node_ref(v) == post_i.get_node_ref(v)` clauses become available inside the by-block. I then re-emit both quantifiers (auto-triggered by `post_i@.contains(v)`) so their disjoint triggers fire pointwise, derive `r1.0 == r2.0` (both equal `pre_self_@[0]`) and `r1.1 == r2.1` (both equal `pre_self_.get_node_ref(pre_self_@[0])`), close `post1_self_@ == post2_self_@` via `=~=` from the common `pre_self_@.skip(1)`, and lift the per-run `get_node_ref` preservation pointwise across the two runs.
